climate_drivers: drought stress;heat waves;winter freeze damage;vineyard age
notes: Kovács, Balling, Bihari et al. (2017) Phytoparasitica 45:21-32. Diplodia seriata isolated with incidence ranging from 50 to 100 percent in surveyed Tokaj vineyards. Soil, topology and vineyard age were major factors; older (>12 years) vineyards much more affected.
Reported among Botryosphaeriaceae isolates in Tokaj 2017 study; precise incidence not extracted.

pathogen: Phaeomoniella chlamydospora; Phaeoacremonium spp.; Fomitiporia mediterranea
climate_drivers: drought stress;heat waves;winter freeze damage;pruning wounds
notes: Phytopathol. Mediterr. (2007) 46:91-95. Surveys 2003-2005 in 11 Hungarian wine regions: 34 percent of plantations had early-decline symptoms with 0.3-2.6 percent vine incidence. ALL vineyards over 12 years old were affected. Most frequently isolated species: Fomitiporia sp., Phaeomoniella chlamydospora, Phomopsis viticola, Botryosphaeria sp., Eutypa lata.
Reported among the most frequently isolated GTD pathogens in 2003-2005 Hungarian survey. No district-level prevalence figure published.

climate_drivers: wet springs;cool wet weather at budburst
notes: Reported among the most frequently isolated GTD pathogens in 2003-2005 Hungarian survey.

Cylindrocarpon sp. was reported among the most frequently isolated fungi from 2-4 year old grapevines in the Hungarian 2003-2005 multi-region trunk disease survey (Phytopathol. Mediterr. 2007 46:91-95), alongside Phaeomoniella chlamydospora, Phaeoacremonium sp. and Fomitiporia sp. No Hungarian-specific incidence figure for black foot specifically (Cylindrocarpon/Dactylonectria) has been published; modern Dactylonectria-level molecular taxonomy (post-2012) is not yet covered in the Hungarian literature.

Hungarian 2003-2005 survey across 11 wine regions (Phytopathol. Mediterr. 2007 46:91-95) found that on 2-4 year old vines, Phaeomoniella chlamydospora and Phaeoacremonium sp. were the most frequently isolated trunk pathogens - i.e. the Petri disease complex is documented in HU young plantings since 2003-2007. Survey reported 0.3-2.6% per-vine incidence of early-decline symptoms (combined Petri/Esca) in young plantations, with 34% of plantations affected; first explicit Hungarian record of P. chlamydospora is the same survey. No subsequent Petri-specific Hungarian incidence study has been published.
Recent (2020) report of Neofabraea kienholzii as a novel GTD pathogen in Hungary.
